text.title: Three-Phase Agentic Workflow for OpenAI Fine-tuning
text.summary: The three phases an agent must execute with explicit human-approval checkpoints: dataset preparation, job submission, and evaluation/promotion decision.

rule.statement: Treat fine-tuning as three discrete phases with human approval gates between phases 1-to-2 and 2-to-3.
rule.rationale: A bad dataset is expensive to discover after training. A bad model is dangerous to deploy without evaluation. Human approval gates prevent automated systems from spending budget or affecting production without review.

list.item: Phase 1 (Dataset prep): validate JSONL, count tokens, estimate cost → human approves or rejects
Phase 2 (Job submission): upload file, create job, poll for completion, download metrics → human approves evaluation
Phase 3 (Evaluation): run fine-tuned model on held-out test set, compare to base model → human decides promote or retrain

rule.statement: Sleep at least 30-60 seconds between poll calls; never poll in a tight loop.
rule.rationale: Tight polling loops hit OpenAI rate limits and provide no value — fine-tuning jobs run for minutes to hours.

rule.statement: Check job.status == "succeeded" before reading job.fine_tuned_model — the field is null until the job succeeds.
rule.rationale: Reading fine_tuned_model before job completion causes an AttributeError that silently breaks the pipeline.

rule.statement: Store fine-tuned model IDs in a config file or database with metadata (base model, dataset version, training date).
rule.rationale: OpenAI model IDs are opaque strings (e.g., ft:gpt-4o-mini::abc123) that become impossible to manage at scale without a registry. Checkpoints expire after 90 days of inactivity.

example.note: Polls every 60 seconds. Returns model ID only on success; raises on failure.

text.reference: LLM quality review prompt for training examples
